tasks.name: Create Feature Branch
tasks.description: Create a feature branch for implementation

tasks.name: Design Interface
tasks.description: Design API specification and data models

arguments.prompt:
Design the interface specification based on this issue:

Title: ${$.get_issue.output.title}
Body: ${$.get_issue.output.body}

Create:
1. API specification (endpoints, request/response formats)
2. Data models (database schema)
3. UI component interfaces

Write the specification to docs/interface-spec.md

tasks.name: Generate Test Cases (TDD: Red)
tasks.description: Generate test cases from interface specification before implementation

arguments.prompt:
Read the interface specification from docs/interface-spec.md.

Test framework: ${$.issue_input.output.test_framework}
Test command: ${$.issue_input.output.test_command}

Generate comprehensive test cases BEFORE implementation (TDD approach):

1. **Database tests**:
   - Schema validation
   - CRUD operations
   - Constraints and relationships

2. **API tests**:
   - Endpoint contracts
   - Request/response validation
   - Error handling
   - Authentication/authorization

3. **UI tests**:
   - Component rendering
   - User interactions
   - Form validation

Use the appropriate file naming convention and syntax for the specified test framework.
These tests should FAIL initially (Red phase of TDD).
Write tests that verify the interface contracts defined in the spec.

tasks.name: Setup Git Worktrees
tasks.description: Create separate worktrees for parallel development to avoid conflicts

args.command: cd ${$.issue_input.output.repo_path} && git add -A && git commit -m 'Add interface spec and tests' --allow-empty && ISSUE=${$.issue_input.output.number} && git worktree add ../worktrees/db -b feature/issue-${ISSUE}-db && git worktree add ../worktrees/api -b feature/issue-${ISSUE}-api && git worktree add ../worktrees/ui -b feature/issue-${ISSUE}-ui && echo '{"db_path": "../worktrees/db", "api_path": "../worktrees/api", "ui_path": "../worktrees/ui"}'

tasks.name: Implementation Loop (TDD: Green)
tasks.description: Implement in separate worktrees until all tests pass (max 5 iterations)

tasks.name: Implement/Fix Database
tasks.description: Implement or fix database layer in dedicated worktree

tasks.name: Implement/Fix API
tasks.description: Implement or fix API layer in dedicated worktree

tasks.name: Implement/Fix UI
tasks.description: Implement or fix UI layer in dedicated worktree

tasks.name: Merge Worktrees
tasks.description: Merge all worktree branches, detecting conflicts

args.command: cd ${$.issue_input.output.repo_path} && ISSUE=${$.issue_input.output.number} && CONFLICT=false && (git merge feature/issue-${ISSUE}-db --no-edit -m 'Merge db' || CONFLICT=true) && (git merge feature/issue-${ISSUE}-api --no-edit -m 'Merge api' || CONFLICT=true) && (git merge feature/issue-${ISSUE}-ui --no-edit -m 'Merge ui' || CONFLICT=true) && if [ "$CONFLICT" = "true" ]; then echo '{"has_conflict": true}'; else echo '{"has_conflict": false}'; fi

tasks.name: Resolve Merge Conflicts
tasks.description: Use Claude Code to intelligently resolve merge conflicts

arguments.prompt:
There are merge conflicts that need to be resolved.

1. Run 'git status' to see conflicting files
2. Read each conflicting file
3. Understand the intent of both changes by reading:
   - docs/interface-spec.md (the specification)
   - The relevant test files
4. Resolve conflicts intelligently by keeping functionality from all branches
5. Stage resolved files with 'git add'
6. Complete the merge with 'git commit'

Ensure the resolved code maintains the interface contract defined in the spec.

tasks.name: Run Tests
tasks.description: Run test suite on merged code

tasks.name: Analyze Test Results
tasks.description: Use AI to intelligently analyze test output and determine pass/fail

arguments.prompt:
Analyze the following test output and determine if ALL tests passed.

Test framework: ${$.issue_input.output.test_framework}
Test output:
```
${$.run_tests.output.stdout}
```

Look for indicators like:
- 'passed', 'ok', 'success', 'PASS' for success
- 'failed', 'error', 'FAIL', 'failure' for failures
- Exit codes, test counts, error messages

IMPORTANT: Your response MUST end with exactly one of these markers:
- ALL_TESTS_PASSED (if all tests passed)
- TESTS_FAILED (if any test failed)

tasks.name: Check Test Result
tasks.description: Parse analyze_results output to determine pass/fail

args.command: echo '${$.analyze_results.output.content[0].text}' | grep -q 'ALL_TESTS_PASSED' && echo '{"passed": true}' || echo '{"passed": false}'

cd ${$.issue_input.output.repo_path} && ISSUE=${$.issue_input.output.number} && git worktree remove ../worktrees/db --force && git worktree remove ../worktrees/api --force && git worktree remove ../worktrees/ui --force && git branch -D feature/issue-${ISSUE}-db feature/issue-${ISSUE}-api feature/issue-${ISSUE}-ui && echo '{"cleaned": true}'

tasks.name: Create Pull Request
tasks.description: Create a pull request for the feature

action.title: ${$.get_issue.output.title}
action.head: feature/issue-${$.issue_input.output.number}
